title: gilia tenuiflora subsp. arenaria
intro: Gilia tenuiflora subsp. arenaria, also known as Yellow Toadshade or Yellow Gilia, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and slender leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gilia tenuiflora subsp. arenaria include its ability to help soothe and calm the respiratory system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate congestion. Herbal preparations of Gilia tenuiflora subsp. arenaria can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
